text:
TheOlympic Gamesis not only a platform for athletes to etch their names in history but also a hotbed of sexual escapades every four years. Regardless of whether you bag a medal, there's a high likelihood of scoring in the bedroom, or in another part of the athletes' village.

The organisers are well aware of this. For the forthcoming Paris Games, they have provided 200,000 male condoms, 20,000 female condoms and 10,000 dental dams for the athletes, and there's no guarantee that will suffice!

As we gear up for the sporting extravaganza, here's a sneak peek into what the frisky competitors can anticipate in the Olympic Village over the next two weeks...

Micah Richards was determined not to let his sole opportunity at the Olympics slip away. We're not referring to Team GB's men's football team at London 2012, but rather his nocturnal activities in the Olympic village.

Despite spending just three nights there, he was resolved to make the most of it. He revealed toGary Linekerand Alan Shearer onThe Rest is Footballpodcast: "We went training, come back to the village, those three nights we were up until 4am.

"You have different campuses, Team GB in one block, Brazil, France, all these countries, and there's a massive room full of condoms. It was like 'What!?' Obviously promoting safe sex and, honest to god, I was on fire.

"I was on absolute fire, honestly. I was a little slimmer, a bit more ripped, bit more lean, it was summertime, walking around with my top off. One of the best times of my life, it was incredible."



One gold medal-winning athlete confessed to having a guard at their door to ensure they could get some much-needed sleep before the big race day. The anonymous sportsperson also had an arrangement with their roommate for moments when one of them needed some privacy.

The athlete revealed to CNN: "My roommate and I would put something on the door so we would know if the other was 'busy'. I feel bad to say it but my coach actually guarded the door the night before me and my roommate were racing [because of our reputations]! But it didn't affect me like that. When I raced after sex I felt light on my feet."

"With a once-in-a-lifetime experience, you want to build memories, whether it's sexual, partying or on the field. I've seen people having sex right out in the open. On the grass, between buildings, people are getting down and dirty."



Why limit the action to the bedroom? At the 2010 Winter Olympics in Vancouver, it was reported that six athletes from Germany, Canada and Austria couldn't resist the heat and dived into a hot tub for what was dubbed a 'whirlpool orgy'. Talk about finding ways to thaw out amidst the icy chill of Canada's west coast winters!

Grindr, the popular gay dating app, experienced a service meltdown in east London as hordes of athletes descended on the city, leading to an influx of eager new users. The sudden spike proved too much for Grindr, causing it to crash and leaving technicians scrambling for a full day to restore the service.

Matthew Syed, once a table tennis whizz at the Barcelona Games in 1992, confessed that he enjoyed more bedroom antics during the event than ever before in his life.

Now a journalist, he confirmed in The Times that the Olympic village is indeed the "sex-fest it is cracked up to be". He reminisced: "My answer is always the same: too right it is. I played my first Games in Barcelona in 1992 and got laid more often in those two and a half weeks than in the rest of my life up to that point.

"That is to say twice, which may not sound a lot, but for a 21-year-old undergraduate with crooked teeth, it was a minor miracle. Barcelona was, for many of us Olympic virgins, as much about sex as it was about sport."



Josh Lakatos, a member of Team USA, inadvertently found himself operating an 'unofficial brothel' during the Sydney Games in 2000. Despite his shooting event having concluded, he was understandably reluctant to leave early.

With spare rooms at his disposal, Lakatos allowed frisky couples free access to the apartment. ESPN reported: "On it went for eight days as scores of Olympians, male and female, trickled into the shooter's house - and that's what everyone called it, Shooters' House - at all hours, stopping by an Oakley duffel bag overflowing with condoms procured from the village's helpful medical clinic."

Eventually, the penny dropped for Lakatos, who said: "I'm running a friggin' brothel in the Olympic Village! I've never witnessed so much debauchery in my entire life."

title: Sex-mad world of Olympic village – hot tub hook-ups and 'brothel' | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Mark Whiley
description: The Olympic village has a reputation for being a hotbed of sexual activity, with athletes from around the world letting off steam away from the competition
